info.name: ChatSaaS Backend API
info.description:
# ChatSaaS Backend API

Complete API collection for the ChatSaaS Backend platform.

## Environment Variables
- `base_url`: API base URL (e.g. `http://localhost:8000`)
- `access_token`: JWT token or API key `csk_xxx` (auto-populated by login/register)
- `workspace_id`: Current workspace ID (auto-populated)
- `workspace_slug`: Current workspace slug (auto-populated)
- `channel_id`: Channel ID (auto-populated)
- `document_id`: Document ID (auto-populated)
- `agent_id`: Agent ID (auto-populated)
- `conversation_id`: Conversation ID (auto-populated)
- `message_id`: Message ID for feedback endpoints
- `session_token`: WebChat session token (auto-populated)
- `widget_id`: WebChat widget ID (auto-populated)
- `invitation_token`: Agent invitation token (auto-populated)
- `canned_response_id`: Canned response ID (auto-populated)
- `rule_id`: Assignment rule ID (auto-populated)
- `outbound_webhook_id`: Outbound webhook ID (auto-populated)
- `webhook_log_id`: Webhook delivery log ID (auto-populated)
- `api_key_id`: API key ID (auto-populated)
- `api_key_raw`: Raw API key returned once on creation — store securely
- `contact_id`: Contact ID for contact management endpoints
- `csat_token`: JWT token from csat_prompt WebSocket event for CSAT submission

## Authentication
Most endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}}`.
API keys (`csk_xxx`) can also be used as Bearer tokens on any authenticated endpoint.

## Trailing Slashes
FastAPI routes are defined with trailing slashes. Always use `/api/channels/` not `/api/channels`.

item.name: Authentication
item.description: User/agent registration, login, and token management.

request.description: Register a new user and create a workspace. Returns JWT access token.
Login as a workspace owner. Returns JWT access token.
Login as an agent. Returns JWT access token.
Accept an agent invitation and set password.
Get current authenticated user and workspace info.
Silently refresh a JWT before it expires. Pass a valid (non-expired) token and receive a new token with a fresh expiry. Call this proactively ~5 minutes before exp to avoid mid-session logouts.

item.name: Channels
item.description: Manage communication channels (WebChat, Telegram, WhatsApp, Instagram). NOTE: list/create routes require trailing slash.

request.description: Create a new WebChat channel. Trailing slash is required.
Create a Telegram bot channel.
Create a WhatsApp Business channel.
Create an Instagram channel.
List all channels for the workspace.
Get a specific channel by ID.
Update channel name and/or active status. All fields optional.
Delete a channel by ID.
Validate credentials for a channel type before creating. Replace 'telegram' in path with: webchat, telegram, whatsapp, instagram.
Get channel statistics summary for the workspace.

item.name: Conversations
item.description: Manage customer conversations, escalations, and agent assignments.

query.description: Filter by status: active, escalated, agent, resolved. Optional.
Only show conversations assigned to current agent (bool, default false).

request.description: List conversations with optional filters.
Get full conversation detail including all messages.
Agent claims an escalated conversation.
Update conversation status. Possible statuses: active, escalated, agent, resolved.
Agent sends a message in a conversation (max 2000 chars).
Get conversation statistics for the workspace.
Get conversations assigned to the current agent.
Create an internal note on a conversation. Only visible to agents/owners, never to customers.
List all internal notes for a conversation.
Submit thumbs-up (positive) or thumbs-down (negative) feedback on an AI response. One feedback per message.
Get feedback for a specific message. Returns null if none submitted.

query.description: Full-text search query across message content (PostgreSQL tsvector).
Filter by channel: webchat, telegram, whatsapp, instagram. Optional.
Filter by status: active, escalated, agent, resolved, paused. Optional.
Filter by assigned agent UUID. Optional.

request.description: Full-text search across conversations using PostgreSQL tsvector. Returns matching conversations with a ts_headline snippet showing the matching context. Requires at least one filter parameter.

query.description: Filter by assigned agent UUID. Optional.

request.description: Export conversations as a streaming CSV file. Growth+ tier required. CSV columns: conversation_id, contact_name, channel_type, status, created_at, resolved_at, message_count, escalated, assigned_agent_name, csat_rating. Response header: Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=conversations_export.csv
Get the CSAT rating submitted for a specific conversation. Returns 404 if no rating exists yet.

item.name: Documents
item.description: Upload and manage knowledge-base documents for AI RAG context. Files are stored in Cloudflare R2. Supported formats: PDF, TXT (max 10MB). The response `file_path` field is the public R2 URL.

formdata.description: File to upload (PDF or TXT, max 10MB). Stored in Cloudflare R2.
Optional custom display name for the document.

request.description: Upload a document to the knowledge base. Text is extracted in-memory then stored in R2. Response includes `file_path` (R2 URL) and `chunks_count`.

query.description: Filter by status: pending, processing, completed, failed. Optional.

request.description: List all documents in the workspace knowledge base. Each document includes `file_path` (R2 URL) and `chunks_count`.
Get a specific document by ID. Returns `file_path` (R2 URL), `status`, and `chunks_count`.
Delete a document and its chunks from the database, and delete the file from Cloudflare R2.
Re-download the file from R2, re-extract text, and regenerate embeddings. R2 URL is preserved (no re-upload). Allowed for `failed` or `completed` documents.
Get document statistics for the workspace (counts by status, total chunks, tier info).

item.name: Agents
item.description: Manage support agents: invite, activate, deactivate, and delete.

request.description: Invite a new agent to the workspace. Sends invitation email.
Accept an agent invitation using the token from the email.
List all agents in the workspace.
List pending (not yet accepted) agent invitations.
Get agent statistics: totals plus per-agent performance breakdown (active conversations, 30-day resolved count, avg CSAT score).
Get info about an invitation token (validity, email, workspace).
Reactivate a previously deactivated agent.
Deactivate an agent (soft disable, not delete).
Resend the invitation email to a pending agent.
Permanently delete an agent from the workspace.
Set current agent availability status. Values: online, offline, busy. Updates heartbeat timestamp — agents are auto-set offline after 5 min of no heartbeat.
Get current agent's status and last heartbeat timestamp.

item.name: WebChat (Public)
item.description: Public WebChat endpoints — no auth required. Used by the widget embedded on customer websites. Supports text and media messages. Files are uploaded to Cloudflare R2 via POST /upload, then referenced in POST /send.

request.description: Get WebChat widget config for a workspace (public). Returns widget_id, business_name, primary_color, position, welcome_message.

body.raw:
{
  "widget_id": "{{widget_id}}",
  "session_token": "{{session_token}}",
  "message": "Hello, I need help with my order.",
  "contact_name": "John Doe",
  "contact_email": "john@example.com",
  "contact_phone": "+919847000000",
  "external_id": "usr_123",
  "metadata": {
    "plan": "premium",
    "total_orders": 12,
    "city": "Thrissur"
  }
}

request.description: Send a text message via WebChat widget. `session_token` is optional on first message (auto-created and returned). Use `external_id` as your internal customer ID for stable identity across sessions. Rate limit: 10 req/min per session (shared with /upload).

Send a media message after uploading via POST /upload. Set `media_url` from the upload response. Either `message` or `media_url` must be provided. `message_type` can be: image, video, audio, document.

formdata.description: Active session token (required — send a text message first to start a session).
File to upload. Supported: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp (5MB), video/mp4 (16MB), audio/mpeg|ogg|aac (16MB), application/pdf (100MB).

request.description: Upload a file from the widget to Cloudflare R2. Returns the R2 URL (`url`), `mime_type`, `size`, `filename`, and `message_type`. Pass the `url` to POST /send as `media_url`. Requires an active session. Rate limit: shared 10 req/min bucket with /send.
Get message history for a WebChat session. Each message includes `msg_type`, `media_url`, `media_mime_type`, `media_filename`, `media_size` for media messages.

item.name: Admin
item.description: Platform admin endpoints. Requires admin/superuser role.

request.description: Get platform-wide stats: total workspaces, users, tier breakdown, recent activity.

query.description: Filter by tier: free, starter, growth, pro. Optional.

request.description: List all workspaces on the platform.

query.description: Only return active users (bool, default false).

request.description: List all users on the platform.
Suspend a user account. Provide the user's UUID.
Unsuspend a previously suspended user account.
Change a workspace's subscription tier. Tiers: free, starter, growth, pro. `reason` is optional.
Get tier change history, optionally filtered by workspace.
Permanently delete a workspace. `confirmation_name` must match the workspace name exactly.
Get platform analytics: message volume, signup trends, escalation statistics.
Aggregate AI feedback (positive/negative) counts per workspace. Super admin only.

item.name: Metrics
item.description: System health and performance metrics.

request.description: Detailed health check: status, timestamp, health breakdown, performance metrics.
Get system-level performance metrics.
Get metrics scoped to a specific workspace.
Get metrics in Prometheus text format for scraping.
Get current alert status and active alert list.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Incoming webhooks from third-party services (Telegram, WhatsApp, Instagram, Stripe, Resend).

header.description: Secret token set when registering the webhook with Telegram.

request.description: Receive message updates from Telegram. Always returns HTTP 200 to prevent retries.
Meta webhook verification challenge. Used when registering the WhatsApp webhook URL in Meta for Developers.

header.description: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body using the app secret.

request.description: Receive message events from WhatsApp Business. Always returns HTTP 200.
Meta webhook verification challenge for Instagram DMs.
Receive DM events from Instagram. Always returns HTTP 200.
Receive billing events from Stripe. Handles: customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted.
Incoming webhook from Resend email service.

item.name: Canned Responses
item.description: Pre-written responses for quick agent replies. Free=5, Starter=10, Growth=50, Pro=unlimited.

request.description: Create a canned response. Owner only. Shortcut must be unique per workspace.
List all canned responses for the workspace. Accessible by owners and agents.
Update a canned response. Owner only.
Delete a canned response. Owner only.

item.name: Assignment Rules
item.description: Automatic conversation routing rules (Pro tier only).

request.description: Create a routing rule. Actions: round_robin, specific_agent, least_loaded. Pro tier only.
List all assignment rules ordered by priority.
Update an assignment rule.
Delete an assignment rule.

item.name: Workspace
item.description: Workspace overview, settings, and AI model configuration.

request.description: Get workspace dashboard stats: conversations today, messages this month, quota remaining.

body.raw:
{
  "fallback_msg": "Sorry, I couldn't understand that. A human agent will assist you shortly.",
  "alert_email": "alerts@mycompany.com",
  "agents_enabled": true,
  "escalation_keywords": ["refund", "angry", "cancel", "lawsuit"],
  "escalation_sensitivity": "medium"
}

request.description: Update workspace settings. All fields optional.
Get current AI provider and model configuration for the workspace.
Set workspace AI provider/model override. Growth+ tier only. Providers: google, openai, groq.

item.name: Outbound Webhooks
item.description: Register external URLs to receive ChatSaaS events. Growth+ tier only.

request.description: Register an outbound webhook. Events: message.received, conversation.escalated, conversation.resolved, conversation.created. Requests are signed with X-ChatSaaS-Signature: sha256=<hmac>.
List all registered outbound webhooks for the workspace.
Enable or disable an outbound webhook, or update its URL/events.
Delete an outbound webhook registration.

query.description: Filter by success: true or false. Optional.

request.description: List delivery attempt logs for an outbound webhook. Returns up to 30 days of history, most recent first. Each log includes status code, response body (truncated to 2000 chars), duration_ms, and is_success flag.
Get full detail of a single delivery log entry, including complete payload and response body.

item.name: API Keys
item.description: Programmatic API access via long-lived API keys (csk_ prefix). Growth+ tier only.

request.description: Create an API key. The raw key (csk_xxx) is returned ONCE — store it securely. It cannot be retrieved again.
List all API keys (shows prefix only, never the full key).
Revoke and delete an API key.

item.name: Billing
item.description: Stripe-powered subscription management.

request.description: Get current subscription status, tier, and Stripe customer ID.
Create a Stripe checkout session for upgrading tier. Returns checkout URL. Tiers: starter, growth, pro.
Create a Stripe customer portal session for managing subscription, invoices, and payment methods.

item.name: Contacts
item.description: Manage customer contacts — search, tag, block, and GDPR-delete. Custom fields gated to Growth+ tier.

query.description: Search by name, email, or phone (ILIKE). Optional.
Filter by blocked status: true or false. Optional.

request.description: List contacts for the workspace with optional search and filters.
Get contact detail including the last 10 conversations.
Update contact details. custom_fields requires Growth+ tier. Fires contact.updated outbound webhook event.
Block a contact. Blocked contacts receive a polite auto-reply and no AI processing or usage counting occurs for their messages.
Unblock a previously blocked contact.
Permanently delete a contact and all their conversations and messages (cascading delete). Use for GDPR erasure requests. This action is irreversible.

item.name: Business Hours
item.description: Configure operating hours and outside-hours auto-response behavior per workspace.

request.description: Get configured business hours for all 7 days of the week (Mon=0 to Sun=6). Returns empty array if not yet configured (treated as always open).

body.raw:
[
  { "day_of_week": 0, "open_time": "09:00", "close_time": "17:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 1, "open_time": "09:00", "close_time": "17:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 2, "open_time": "09:00", "close_time": "17:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 3, "open_time": "09:00", "close_time": "17:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 4, "open_time": "09:00", "close_time": "17:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 5, "open_time": "10:00", "close_time": "14:00", "is_closed": false, "timezone": "America/New_York" },
  { "day_of_week": 6, "open_time": "00:00", "close_time": "00:00", "is_closed": true, "timezone": "America/New_York" }
]

request.description: Upsert business hours for all 7 days. day_of_week: 0=Monday, 6=Sunday. is_closed=true marks the day as fully closed regardless of open/close times. timezone must be a valid IANA timezone (e.g. America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Kolkata).

Set the auto-reply message and behavior when a message arrives outside business hours. Behaviors: inform_and_continue (reply then continue to AI), inform_and_pause (reply and pause conversation until business hours).

item.name: CSAT - Public (No Auth)
item.description: Customer-facing CSAT endpoints. No authentication required — secured via time-limited JWT in token parameter.

request.description: Validate a CSAT token and get conversation context for the rating form. Token is a JWT with 72h expiry delivered via WebSocket csat_prompt event. Returns 400 if token is expired or invalid.
Submit a CSAT rating (1-5) for a resolved conversation. Token must be the JWT from the csat_prompt WebSocket event. One rating per conversation. Fires csat.submitted outbound webhook event.

item.name: CSAT Metrics
item.description: Workspace-level CSAT analytics. Growth+ required for date-range trend.

query.description: ISO date filter start. Optional, Growth+ only for trend data.
ISO date filter end. Optional, Growth+ only for trend data.

request.description: Get CSAT metrics: average score (1-5), total_rated, total_resolved, response_rate (%). Growth+ tier additionally returns a daily/weekly trend array when date_from/date_to are provided.

item.name: WebSocket (Reference)
item.description:
WebSocket connection documentation. Use a WebSocket client (e.g. wscat) to connect.

Connect: ws://{{base_url}}/ws/{{workspace_id}}?token={{access_token}}

Incoming message types:
- ping
- subscribe
- get_stats
- get_conversations
- get_agents

Outgoing event types:
- connection_established
- pong
- escalation
- agent_claim
- new_message
- conversation_status_change
- system_notification

request.description: Check WebSocket server health and connection stats.
List active WebSocket connections for a workspace.
Broadcast a message to all WebSocket connections in a workspace.
Create a new AI agent (starts as draft). Requires Starter tier+.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Order Support Bot",
  "system_prompt": "You are a helpful order support assistant. Use available tools to look up order status.",
  "persona_name": "Aria",
  "persona_tone": "friendly",
  "first_message": "Hi! I'm Aria. How can I help with your order?",
  "escalation_trigger": "low_confidence",
  "escalation_message": "Let me connect you with a team member.",
  "confidence_threshold": 0.7,
  "max_turns": 10,
  "token_budget": 8000
}

request.description: List all AI agents in the workspace.
Get full agent details including tools and guardrails.
Update agent settings. Partial updates supported.
Validate and go live (sets is_draft=false). Requires system_prompt, escalation_message, and at least one tool.
Delete agent and all its tools, guardrails, and channel assignments.
Add an HTTP tool the agent can call. name must be snake_case.
List all tools for an agent.
Update a tool. Partial updates supported.
Delete a tool.
Dry-run a tool call without quota charges. Verifies your endpoint is reachable.
Add a safety guardrail. rule_type: forbidden_topic | forbidden_action | required_escalation
List all guardrails for an agent.
Delete a guardrail.
Assign agent to a channel. The agent must be published first.
Remove channel assignment. Channel falls back to RAG mode.
Test the agent interactively. No quota charge. Response includes debug block with tool_called, tokens, cost, model_used.
Clear sandbox session history for this agent.
Token usage, cost breakdown, conversation stats, tool call success rate.
Switch pipeline mode (rag|ai_agent) and set provider/model. Requires Growth+ tier.
Get current AI pipeline configuration (mode, provider, model).
